findings.replacement: SHA-256 / SHA-3-256 (or SHA-384+ for long-term assurance)
findings.notes: Collisions trivially producible. Unsafe for any security purpose. If used as a non-cryptographic content hash (cache keys, ETags, content addressing), changing to SHA-256 will alter output bytes — coordinate the change with downstream consumers.
